relations.note: Singles out the masthead gradient from the approved set for logo pairing.
Direction rule applies to the approved gradients when used in masthead/banner.

effect.reserved_for: the approved gradient set (Brand Blue → Dark Navy, Sky Blue → Deep Blue, Sunshine → 75% Gold, Golden Sand → White → Light Blue, Golden Sand → 75% Gold → Sunshine, masthead Sunshine → light gold)
effect.subject: gradients

quotes: Used as backgrounds, in combination with the wave graphic, and with the LISRAYA logo mark. Approved values only.

rules.intent: Preserve brand consistency by restricting gradients to the approved palette.
rules.rule_text: Only the approved gradient set may be used; never invent gradients. Approved gradients: Brand Blue → Dark Navy (#00529b → #011E45, dark backgrounds, blue masthead/waves); Sky Blue → Deep Blue (#358CCB → #003D74, blue backgrounds, waves); Sunshine → 75% Gold (#faa31b → Gold @75%, warm backgrounds); Golden Sand → White → Light Blue (#FEF1C8 → #ffffff → #E6F0F9, soft light backgrounds); Golden Sand → 75% Gold → Sunshine (#FEF1C8 → Gold @75% → #faa31b, warm light-to-saturated backgrounds); Sunshine → light gold (#faa31b → ≈#ffe05c) as the masthead gradient. Gradients are used as backgrounds, in combination with the wave graphic, and with the LISRAYA logo mark, using approved values only.

quotes: Masthead gradient — the gradient to use with the logo mark for contrast; mark sits at the lightest end
Masthead gradient — the gradient to use with the logo mark for contrast; mark sits at the lightest end

rules.intent: Ensure logo legibility against the masthead gradient.
rules.rule_text: The Sunshine → light gold gradient (#faa31b → ≈#ffe05c) is the masthead gradient to use with the logo mark for contrast; the logo mark sits at the lightest end.

quotes: Gradients run horizontally in masthead/banner applications (dark/saturated → light, left → right as shown).

rules.intent: Maintain consistent gradient orientation in masthead and banner artwork.
rules.rule_text: In masthead/banner applications, gradients run horizontally, from dark/saturated to light (left → right as shown).
